rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHORTALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Flowserve Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHORTALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Carrier Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied for violation of CRC 3.1350(d). Even if the court considered defendant¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s negligence, strict liability and false representation causes of action and claim for punitive damages, it would be denied. It would be denied as to negligence, strict liability and false representation for the same reasons summary judgment is denied. The only ground on which defendant based adjudication of the false representation cause of action was the same ground on which defendant based summary judgment. Adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages would be denied because defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHORTALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Reliable Pipe and Supply Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (negligence and strict liability) and off calendar as to issues 2, 3 and 4 (false misrepresentation, premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages). Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation inserts) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay are overruled. Plaintiff has dismissed his false misrepresentation and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Frank Bonetti Plumbing Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McClain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Cantley's deposition testimony and declaration would create a triable issue whether Mr. McClain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation and fireproofing) attributable to defendant. (Plaintiffs' response to defendant's separate statement, facts nos. 9-11, 14.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. on Thursday January 17, 2013 in Department 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Johnson Controls, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the sophisticated user issue and the negligence and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action and off calendar as to the strict liability cause of action. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. However, even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Cantley's declaration creates a triable issue whether the sophisticated user defense applies. (Plaintiffs' response to defendant's Separate Statement, facts nos. 6-11; plaintiffs' Separate Statement, facts nos. 5-12.) Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to decedent such that the negligence cause of action should be dismissed. O'Neil v. Crane Co. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 335 and Davis v. Foster Wheeler Energy Corp. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 731 are inapplicable. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs' premises owner/contractor liability cause of action should be dismissed. Plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. on Thursday January 17, 2013 in Department 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Defendant Therma Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claim, namely defendant failed to submit evidence to demonstrate that the entity identified as Therma was not this defendant. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, the website printout and defendant's responses to General Order No. 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether the entity identified as Therma was this defendant. (See plaintiffs' Separate Statement, facts nos. 1, 5.) If a hearing is requested, it will be 9:30 a.m. on Thursday January 17, 2013 in Department 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
